mnemonic: UNIX SYSTEM PROGRAMMING: Remembering key concepts
keyPoints: UNIX System Calls: fork(), exec(), wait() are fundamental for process management.
File I/O: open(), read(), write(), close() are essential for file operations.
Signals: signal(), kill() for inter-process communication and control.
Pipes and FIFOs: pipe(), mkfifo() for process communication.
Sockets: socket(), bind(), listen(), accept() for network programming.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check return values of system calls for errors.
Not handling signals properly, leading to unexpected process behavior.
Misusing file descriptors, causing resource leaks or incorrect I/O.
Ignoring race conditions in concurrent programming.
Overlooking buffer sizes, leading to overflow or inefficient I/O.
